records.title: [OBS-012] Create agent mission packet requirement for observability compliance
records.objective: Ensure every dispatched agent knows how to log, claim files, release files, and halt safely.
records.scope: _(no explicit scope - see objective + eval requirements)_
records.acceptance:
- [ ] Mission packet template includes before/during/after observability actions.
- [ ] Agent handoffs require file claims before mutation.
- [ ] Stop conditions include collision, dirty repo ambiguity, schema validation failure, and secret detection.
- [ ] Acceptance criteria require release of claimed files.

records.title: [OBS-011] Add event lifecycle tools for find/update/archive
records.objective: Support lookup, status updates, and long-term archival for JSONL event history.
records.scope: _(no explicit scope - see objective + eval requirements)_
records.acceptance:
- [ ] find_event.py can locate an event by event_id.
- [ ] update_event_status.py appends status update records instead of mutating history.
- [ ] Archive policy is documented.
- [ ] Reports compute latest status from appended lifecycle events.

records.title: [OBS-010] Generate recurring observability reports and learning candidates

records.scope: _(no explicit scope - see objective + eval requirements)_
records.acceptance:
- [ ] generate_observability_report.py writes a dated markdown report.
- [ ] propose_learnings.py identifies repeated error signatures.
- [ ] Report includes unresolved high/critical events, repeated signatures, noisy files, and recommended next work.
- [ ] Learning candidates are appended to LEARNINGS_LOG.md or staged for review.

records.title: [OBS-009] Add secret scan gate to pre-commit and CI

records.title: [OBS-006] Add Git state snapshots and last-known-good flow
records.objective: Capture branch, commit, dirty state, staged files, modified files, and untracked files before/after agent work.
records.scope: _(no explicit scope - see objective + eval requirements)_

- [ ] check_dirty_state.py exits non-zero when repo is dirty if strict mode is enabled.
- [ ] update_last_known_good.py records a clean validated checkpoint.

records.title: [OBS-005] Adopt harness_run.py as terminal command wrapper

records.scope: _(no explicit scope - see objective + eval requirements)_

- [ ] Wrapper preserves original command exit code.

records.title: [OBS-004] Route critical/high events to incidents, collisions, and queue items

records.scope: _(no explicit scope - see objective + eval requirements)_
records.acceptance:
- [ ] Critical events append to INCIDENT_LOG.md.
- [ ] file_collision events append to COLLISION_REGISTER.md.
- [ ] medium/high unresolved errors create ERROR_REMEDIATION_QUEUE.md entries.
- [ ] Each downstream record links back to the source event_id.

records.scope: _(no explicit scope - see objective + eval requirements)_
records.acceptance:
- [ ] Invalid severity, status, category, or event_type fails validation.
- [ ] Missing source metadata fails validation.
- [ ] Malformed JSONL exits non-zero.
- [ ] Validation command is documented in QUICKSTART.md.

records.title: [OBS-002] Enable file claim/release collision control
records.objective: Wire claim_files.py and release_files.py into agent and IDE workflows so multiple writers cannot silently edit the same file.
records.scope: _(no explicit scope - see objective + eval requirements)_
records.acceptance:
- [ ] First writer can claim one or more files.
- [ ] Second writer claiming same file receives non-zero exit code.
- [ ] Collision is logged and routed to COLLISION_REGISTER.md.
- [ ] Release clears active writer entries.

records.title: PR size and change-risk analysis gate
records.objective: Add a PR/change-set sizing and risk gate so oversized or high-risk changes are flagged before GitHub promotion.
records.scope:
- Count changed files, changed lines, deleted lines, generated files, and protected-path touches.
- Classify risk by size, file type, subsystem, and protected area.
- Warn or fail based on configured thresholds.
- Emit risk summary into closeout and PR handoff artifacts.
records.owner:
Sentinel + Auditor

Close this issue when the bead is marked done.
records.acceptance:
- [ ] Changed-file and line-count metrics are captured.
- [ ] Protected path touches are detected.
- [ ] Configurable thresholds exist for warn/fail behavior.
- [ ] Large PR warning appears before push/PR creation.
- [ ] Risk result is saved to audit output.

records.title: Mirror agent queue to GitHub issues and mutations to PRs (Phase 3)
records.objective: Provide full bidirectional visibility between the internal agent handoff queue and GitHub: queue items surface as issues so humans can track them, and mutations (state changes, closes, PR links) are reflected back on GitHub in near-real time.
records.scope:
- Queue-to-issue mirroring (Phase 3a): already shipped in `scripts/sync_queue_to_github.py` and PR #53.
- Mutation-to-PR mirroring (Phase 3b): when a bead transitions (claim, close, fail), open or update a linked GitHub PR or comment.
- Bidirectional close sync (Phase 3c): closing a GH issue marks the bead done; bead marked done closes the GH issue.
- Dedup guard: re-runs must not create duplicate issues or PRs.
- Audit trail: every sync action appended to `07_LOGS_AND_AUDIT/queue_sync/history.jsonl`.
records.acceptance:
- [ ] `python scripts/sync_queue_to_github.py --execute` creates one GH issue per open queue bead and skips existing ones (no duplicates on re-run).
- [ ] When a bead is claimed or closed, a corresponding GitHub PR or issue comment is created/updated with the new state within one sync cycle.
- [ ] Closing a GH issue tagged `agent-queue` triggers bead done marking (or logs a pending mutation for the next harvest cycle).
- [ ] Running `sync_queue_to_github.py --execute --close-done` closes GH issues whose beads are marked done, without touching open beads.
- [ ] Sync history is appended to `07_LOGS_AND_AUDIT/queue_sync/history.jsonl` with timestamp, action, bead_id, and issue number on every --execute run.
- [ ] intake_issues.py accepts issue #51 as valid (Acceptance checks section present) and seed_issues_to_beads.py places it in the Queue Infrastructure epic.
